negative_variants.description: Analog Primitive Resistor Instance: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to Instantiate the resistor primitive as an internal analog primitive.

Analog Primitive Resistor Instance: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Instantiate a Spectre analog resistor primitive inside a Verilog-A module fail.
Analog Primitive Resistor Instance: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Analog Primitive Resistor Instance: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for Instantiate the resistor primitive as an internal analog primitive fails.

Analog Primitive Resistor Instance: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Instantiate a Spectre analog resistor primitive inside a Verilog-A module fail.
